full_prompt: Design a transverse watertight bulkhead panel representative of a naval-engineering research benchmark for a 30-meter aluminum high-speed patrol craft, sized per ABS Rules for Building and Classing High-Speed Craft (Part 3). Panel outer dimensions 2.2 m height x 2.8 m width, mounted between two longitudinal bulkheads and the hull plating on each side, 4.0 m above the design waterline. Material: marine-grade AL 5083-H116 (Young's modulus 70.3 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 228 MPa, ultimate 317 MPa, density 2660 kg/m^3). Plate thickness 6 mm with vertical stiffeners on 600 mm pitch, TB-section flat-bar 6 mm x 75 mm. Load cases: (LC1) hydrostatic head at flooding case: 4 m water column = 40 kPa on panel; (LC2) slamming pressure per ABS HSC Part 3: 60 kPa (at panel station 4 m above WL) applied as dynamic-equivalent static; (LC3) naval grade-B shock equivalent 10 g vertical + 6 g athwartship simultaneous (MIL-S-901D analog representation); (LC4) fire-safety deformation limit: panel must survive compartment fire with thermal gradient 100 C across panel for 30 minutes without exceeding 0.5% elastic strain; (LC5) fatigue: 10^7 cycles of +/- 10 kPa wave-induced pressure at 0.1 Hz; (LC6) anti-shrapnel load: 50 kN point load on a 100 mm x 100 mm patch simulating blast fragment. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises panel + stiffeners under LC1-LC3 <= 152 MPa (yield 228 / 1.5); (R2) panel mid-span out-of-plane deflection under LC2 <= panel_width / 200 = 14 mm; (R3) shock LC3 no yield, first natural mode of panel above 30 Hz; (R4) fatigue alternating stress at stiffener toe welds <= 40 MPa (BS 7608 Class F at 1e7 cycles); (R5) panel mass <= 180 kg; (R6) linear buckling eigenvalue for panel under in-plane compression from deck loads >= 3.0. Deliverables: STEP, FEA per case, fatigue summary, buckling eigenvalue, modal, and mass report.
short_prompt:
Design a transverse watertight bulkhead panel for a 30-meter aluminum high-speed patrol craft, mounted 4.0 m above the design waterline between two longitudinal bulkheads and the hull plating. Material class: marine-grade AL 5083-H116. Qualify per ABS Rules for Building and Classing High-Speed Craft (Part 3) for hydrostatic / slamming, BS 7608 Class F for stiffener-toe weld fatigue, and a MIL-S-901D grade-B shock analog.

Envelope: panel 2.2 m H x 2.8 m W, plate 6 mm with vertical TB-section stiffeners (6 x 75 mm) on 600 mm pitch.

Load cases.
- LC1 hydrostatic flooding: 4 m water column = 40 kPa.
- LC2 slamming per ABS HSC: 60 kPa applied dynamic-equivalent static.
- LC3 grade-B shock: 10 g vertical + 6 g athwartship simultaneous.
- LC4 compartment fire: 100 C through-panel gradient for 30 min, limited to 0.5% elastic strain.
- LC5 wave fatigue: 1e7 cycles of +/- 10 kPa at 0.1 Hz.
- LC6 anti-shrapnel: 50 kN on a 100 x 100 mm patch.

prompt.title: Naval research aluminum ship bulkhead panel
prompt.brief: 5083-H116 bulkhead for 30 m HSC patrol craft per ABS HSC Part 3.

notes.needs_rulebook_fetch_reason: Naval-engineering research benchmark has no fixed annual rulebook; numerics anchored to ABS HSC Part 3 + grade-B shock analogs.
